Decision trees, linear regression, and sentiment analysis are supervised methods that require labeled training data with known outputs.

question: Which performance metrics evaluate the effectiveness of a healthcare classification system?

options.A: Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), and R-squared
options.B: Precision, Recall, and F1-Score
options.C: Bias and Variance
options.D: Throughput, Latency, and Uptime

rationale: Precision, Recall, and F1-Score are the standard evaluation metrics for classification systems — especially critical in healthcare where false positives and false negatives have different clinical implications. MAE/RMSE/R-squared are regression metrics. Bias/Variance are model complexity concepts. Throughput/Latency/Uptime are system performance metrics.

question: What are the key differences between real-time inference and batch inference? (Select two)

options.A: Real-time inference processes data in large batches; batch inference processes individual data points immediately
options.B: Real-time inference is used for immediate predictions with low latency; batch inference processes large data volumes at once, often with higher latency
options.C: Real-time inference follows a synchronous execution mode; batch inference follows an asynchronous execution mode
options.D: Batch inference follows API-based invocation; real-time inference follows schedule-based invocation
options.E: Batch inference follows synchronous execution; real-time inference follows asynchronous execution

rationale: Real-time inference: immediate low-latency predictions, synchronous execution (request → wait → response) (B, C). Batch inference: processes large datasets in a scheduled/delayed batch, asynchronous execution (submit → process in background → retrieve results later) (B, C). Options A and D swap the definitions. Option E reverses synchronous/asynchronous.

question: A retail company wants business analysts to use natural language to build BI dashboards without coding. Which solution?

options.A: Amazon Q Developer
options.B: Amazon Q in Connect
options.C: Amazon Q Business
options.D: Amazon Q in QuickSight

rationale: Amazon Q in QuickSight allows business analysts to use natural language to query data, generate visualizations, and build BI dashboards without SQL or coding expertise. Q Developer is for software development. Q in Connect assists customer service agents. Q Business provides enterprise knowledge access — not BI dashboard creation.

question: Which two governance strategies should an AI team prioritize for ethical deployment? (Select two)

options.A: Implement robust auditing processes for AI outputs
options.B: Use only open-source models without further evaluation
options.C: Allow unrestricted fine-tuning by all team members
options.D: Establish ethical AI guidelines for developers to follow
options.E: Skip documentation for faster deployment

rationale: Robust auditing processes for AI outputs ensure accountability and help detect bias, errors, and unintended behaviors (A). Establishing ethical AI guidelines creates a consistent framework for responsible development and deployment decisions (D). Using open-source models without evaluation ignores risks. Unrestricted fine-tuning and skipping documentation undermine governance and transparency.

question: What is a key difference between labeled and unlabeled data in machine learning?

options.A: Labeled data includes annotations providing meaning and is used for supervised learning; unlabeled data lacks annotations and is used for unsupervised learning
options.B: Labeled data can only be used for unsupervised learning; unlabeled data is for supervised learning
options.C: Labeled data is more complex due to lack of structure; unlabeled data is easier because it has clear annotations
options.D: Labeled data consists of raw information with no tags; unlabeled data includes tags that provide meaning

rationale: Labeled data = raw data plus annotations/tags that provide ground truth (e.g., 'cat' label on an image), used for supervised learning. Unlabeled data = raw data without annotations, used for unsupervised learning to discover patterns. Options B, C, D either swap the definitions or incorrectly characterize the data types.

question: An e-learning chatbot needs to tailor responses dynamically based on the user's age group using Amazon Bedrock. Which solution?

options.A: Perform model re-training for tailoring responses based on user age
options.B: Implement dynamic prompt engineering to customize responses based on user characteristics like age
options.C: Leverage Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to customize responses based on user characteristics
options.D: Perform fine-tuning to adjust style or tone based on user age

rationale: Dynamic prompt engineering is the most efficient and flexible approach for real-time personalization — include the user's age group in the system prompt to guide the model's tone, vocabulary, and content choices. Model re-training and fine-tuning require expensive offline training runs and aren't dynamic. RAG retrieves documents but doesn't inherently customize based on user demographics.

question: What correctly describes the difference between multi-class and multi-label classification?

options.A: Multi-class is for image data only; multi-label is for text data only
options.B: Multi-class classification does not require labeled data; multi-label requires labeled data
options.C: Multi-label classification allows each instance to belong to multiple classes simultaneously; multi-class restricts each instance to one class only
options.D: Multi-class assigns each instance to exactly one of several possible classes; multi-label assigns each instance to one or more classes simultaneously

rationale: Multi-class classification: each instance is assigned to exactly ONE of K mutually exclusive classes (e.g., a movie is 'Action' OR 'Comedy' OR 'Drama'). Multi-label classification: each instance can have multiple labels simultaneously (e.g., a movie can be 'Action' AND 'Comedy' AND 'Adventure'). Option D is the most precise and complete statement.

question: A financial company wants to measure the overall percentage of correct outcomes from their ML model. Which metric?

options.A: F1 Score, which considers both precision and recall
options.B: Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), which calculates the square root of squared differences
options.C: R-squared, which indicates the proportion of variance explained
options.D: Accuracy, which measures the proportion of correctly predicted instances out of the total instances

rationale: Accuracy = (TP + TN) / Total = the proportion of all correctly predicted outcomes. This directly answers 'overall percentage of correct outcomes.' F1 is for imbalanced classification problems. RMSE and R-squared are regression metrics measuring prediction error, not classification correctness.

question: A company needs to redact PII from support tickets before processing them for search indexes. Which AWS service?

options.A: Amazon Comprehend
options.B: Amazon Textract
options.C: Amazon Lex
options.D: Amazon Translate

rationale: Amazon Comprehend can detect and redact Personally Identifiable Information (PII) entities — names, addresses, phone numbers, SSNs — from text documents, making it the right tool for preprocessing support tickets before indexing. Textract extracts text from scanned documents. Lex builds conversational interfaces. Translate converts between languages.

question: A company generates summaries from a prompt without providing specific examples. What prompting technique is this?

options.A: Negative prompting
options.B: Chain-of-thought prompting
options.C: Zero-shot prompting
options.D: Few-shot prompting

rationale: Zero-shot prompting gives the model an instruction or task description without providing any examples — the model relies entirely on its pre-trained knowledge to complete the task. Few-shot provides examples. Chain-of-thought asks for step-by-step reasoning. Negative prompting specifies what to avoid.

question: What is the primary distinction between discriminative and generative models in generative AI?

options.A: Discriminative models use both labeled and unlabeled data; generative models use only labeled data
options.B: Generative models focus on generating new data from learned patterns; discriminative models classify data by distinguishing between different classes
options.C: Discriminative models are only for text classification; generative models are only for image classification
options.D: Discriminative models generate new data; generative models are only for classification

rationale: Discriminative models (logistic regression, SVMs, most classifiers) learn the boundary between classes — they model P(y|x) and predict class labels. Generative models (GANs, VAEs, diffusion models, LLMs) learn the underlying data distribution — they model P(x) and can generate new samples. Options C and D swap the definitions.

question: Which CORRECT statement describes Amazon SageMaker Model Cards?

options.A: Model Cards can be customized to meet the business needs
options.B: The purpose of a Model Card is to describe technical deployment requirements
options.C: Model Cards describe how a model should be used in a production environment
options.D: Model Cards cannot be created for models not trained on Amazon SageMaker

rationale: SageMaker Model Cards are flexible and customizable — they can include information relevant to the business context: intended use, risk rating, training details, evaluation results, ethical considerations, and custom observations. They can be created for ANY ML model, not just those trained on SageMaker (D is false). They document model governance information, not deployment technical specs.

question: A healthcare company needs to deploy an ML model for continuous, low-latency predictions with persistent endpoints during patient check-ins. What deployment option?

options.A: Serverless inference
options.B: Asynchronous inference
options.C: Batch transform
options.D: Real-time hosting services

rationale: Real-time hosting services (SageMaker endpoints) provide persistent, always-on endpoints that serve individual prediction requests with low latency — exactly what's needed for patient check-ins requiring immediate responses. Serverless inference has cold start latency. Asynchronous inference queues requests for large payloads. Batch transform processes datasets offline.

question: Which use case is addressed by Amazon Personalize?

options.A: Generate recommendations for items similar to an item the user is viewing
options.B: Extract layout elements like paragraphs, titles, and lists from documents
options.C: Offer personalized experiences for mobile subscriber activities like SIM activation
options.D: Provide enterprise search powered by machine learning

rationale: Amazon Personalize can generate item-to-item similarity recommendations — when a user views a product, Personalize suggests similar items they're likely to be interested in. Document layout extraction is Amazon Textract. Telecom personalization (SIM activation etc.) is a different managed service. Enterprise ML-powered search is Amazon Kendra.

question: A large e-commerce company's LLM-based customer service assistant faces prompt engineering attacks from malicious users. Best mitigation approach?

options.A: Disable user-generated inputs and rely solely on internal data
options.B: Monitor the length of prompts to prevent overly long inputs
options.C: Create a prompt template that teaches the LLM to detect and resist attack patterns
options.D: Implement input validation and guardrails to filter potentially malicious inputs

rationale: Creating prompt templates with explicit instructions that help the LLM recognize and resist adversarial manipulation is the primary prompt-level defense against prompt injection attacks. Combined with Guardrails for Amazon Bedrock, this provides layered protection. Disabling user inputs breaks functionality. Length monitoring doesn't detect semantic attacks. Input validation is complementary but option C specifically addresses prompt-level defense.

question: A healthcare company needs to explain how input features like age and blood pressure contribute to patient outcome predictions. Which SageMaker service?

options.A: Amazon SageMaker Clarify
options.B: Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth
options.C: Amazon SageMaker Canvas
options.D: Amazon SageMaker JumpStart

rationale: SageMaker Clarify provides feature attribution using SHAP values, showing exactly how each input feature contributes to individual model predictions. This is the explainability capability needed for regulatory compliance in healthcare — understanding why the model predicted a certain outcome for a specific patient.

question: A tech company uses SageMaker Studio and needs supported IDEs for coding, testing, and debugging. Which option addresses this?

options.A: AWS Cloud9
options.B: Code Editor (based on Code-OSS/VS Code)
options.C: RStudio
options.D: JupyterLab

rationale: Amazon SageMaker Studio supports JupyterLab as one of its primary built-in IDEs for writing, testing, and debugging ML code. SageMaker Studio also supports Code Editor (VS Code-based) and RStudio for R users, but JupyterLab is the most prominently supported and commonly used environment within Studio.

question: An e-commerce company wants to fine-tune a Bedrock Foundation Model using a labeled training dataset. Which best-fit solution?

options.A: Make a public copy of the base FM and train it using the labeled dataset
options.B: Discard the selected FM and create a new model from scratch
options.C: Make a separate private copy of the base FM and train this private copy using the labeled dataset
options.D: Train the base FM itself using the labeled training dataset

rationale: Amazon Bedrock fine-tuning creates a PRIVATE copy of the selected base FM and trains that copy with your labeled dataset — it stays in your account and is not shared. You cannot directly train the base FM (D). Building from scratch in Bedrock isn't the supported pattern. A public copy (A) would expose your customized model to others.

question: A call center introduced a chatbot to help employees respond to customer inquiries. Which metric should be monitored to evaluate success?

options.A: Average Call Duration
options.B: First-Call Resolution Rate
options.C: Average Chat Sessions per Day
options.D: Chat API Calls per Day

rationale: First-Call Resolution Rate measures how often customer issues are resolved on the first interaction — the most direct indicator of chatbot effectiveness in improving call center efficiency and customer satisfaction. Average Call Duration tracks time but not resolution quality. Chat sessions and API calls measure volume, not effectiveness.

question: What summarizes the differences between Guardrails for Amazon Bedrock and watermark detection for Amazon Bedrock?

options.A: Both Guardrails and watermark detection help identify if an image was created by the Amazon Titan Image Generator
options.B: Guardrails helps control interaction between users and FMs by filtering undesirable content; watermark detection identifies if an image was created by the Amazon Titan Image Generator
options.C: Watermark detection filters harmful content; Guardrails identifies AI-generated images
options.D: Both Guardrails and watermark detection help control interaction by filtering harmful content

rationale: Guardrails = real-time content control layer that filters harmful, inappropriate, or off-topic content and redacts PII — a safety and alignment tool. Watermark detection = identifies whether images were generated by Amazon Titan Image Generator — a provenance and authenticity verification tool. They serve completely different purposes.

question: A company wants employees to use natural language to convert plain English commands into SQL queries. Which solution?

options.A: WaveNet, suitable for generating SQL from text
options.B: GPT (Generative Pre-trained Transformer) to interpret natural language and generate SQL queries
options.C: Amazon Comprehend to interpret natural language and generate SQL
options.D: ResNet (Residual Neural Network) for generating text-based outputs like SQL

rationale: GPT (Generative Pre-trained Transformer) is a natural language understanding and generation model capable of interpreting plain English commands and generating structured outputs like SQL queries. WaveNet is a speech synthesis model. Amazon Comprehend analyzes text for NLP insights but doesn't generate SQL. ResNet is a computer vision architecture.

question: A healthcare company needs human review incorporated at multiple ML lifecycle stages to improve model accuracy. What do you suggest?

options.A: Amazon SageMaker Feature Store
options.B: Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth
options.C: Amazon SageMaker Model Monitor
options.D: Amazon SageMaker Role Manager

rationale: SageMaker Ground Truth provides human-in-the-loop (HITL) capabilities for data labeling and human review across multiple stages of the ML lifecycle, incorporating expert human input to improve data quality and model accuracy. Feature Store manages features. Model Monitor detects drift in deployed models. Role Manager controls access permissions.

question: A call center chatbot needs to suggest missing words in customer sentences to help resolve issues faster. Which model?

options.A: Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT) based Model
options.B: Rule-based NLP Model
options.C: Prescriptive AI Model
options.D: Clustering Model

rationale: BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers) is specifically designed for masked language modeling — predicting missing/masked words using bidirectional context from both left and right of the gap. This was BERT's original pre-training objective, making it ideal for suggesting missing words in sentences.

question: Which AWS service/feature offers a choice of high-performing Foundation Models with the ability to privately customize them with your data?

options.A: Amazon Q Developer
options.B: Amazon Bedrock
options.C: Amazon Q in QuickSight
options.D: AWS Inferentia

rationale: Amazon Bedrock provides access to a catalog of high-performing foundation models from leading AI providers (Anthropic, Amazon, Meta, Mistral, Cohere, Stability AI) and allows you to privately fine-tune and customize these models with your own data — all within your AWS account. Q Developer is for coding. Q in QuickSight is for BI. Inferentia is a hardware accelerator chip.

question: A global e-commerce company is concerned about inappropriate or malicious content generation from its Bedrock chatbot. Most effective approach?

options.A: Retrain the underlying FM from scratch to prevent exposure
options.B: Develop a custom API layer that monitors and filters all model outputs
options.C: Instruct the model through explicit system prompt guidelines to stay on topic and refuse harmful requests
options.D: Adjust the model hyperparameters to safeguard the output

rationale: Using explicit system prompt instructions is the most practical, immediate, and cost-effective defense — tell the model what topics to focus on, what to refuse, and how to handle adversarial inputs. Combined with Guardrails for Amazon Bedrock for automated filtering, this provides layered protection. Retraining is extremely costly. Custom API filtering adds latency and complexity. Hyperparameters don't control content safety.

question: A healthcare company needs to generate synthetic patient data preserving statistical properties. Which method?

options.A: Support Vector Machines (SVMs) for generating synthetic data
options.B: Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) best-suited for synthetic data generation
options.C: WaveNet, a deep generative model for general-purpose synthetic data
options.D: Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) for creating realistic synthetic data while preserving statistical properties

rationale: GANs are specifically designed to generate realistic synthetic data that mirrors the statistical distribution of the training data — a generator creates realistic samples while a discriminator ensures quality. This makes GANs ideal for synthetic patient data that preserves statistical properties for downstream ML use. SVMs are classifiers. CNNs process images. WaveNet is for audio synthesis.

question: A healthcare organization needs guidance on each model's intended use and risk assessment. Which AWS tool?

options.A: Amazon SageMaker Canvas
options.B: Amazon SageMaker Clarify
options.C: Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth
options.D: Amazon SageMaker Model Cards

rationale: SageMaker Model Cards document each ML model's intended use cases, risk rating, training details, performance metrics, evaluation results, and ethical considerations — providing exactly the guidance and risk assessment documentation needed for responsible AI deployment in sensitive domains like healthcare.

question: Which is a key use case addressed by Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler?

options.A: Build ML models with no code
options.B: Store and share features used for model development
options.C: Monitor the quality of a model
options.D: Fix bias by balancing the dataset

rationale: SageMaker Data Wrangler includes built-in analyses and transformations for detecting and fixing data imbalances and biases — including oversampling, undersampling, and SMOTE techniques to balance datasets before training. No-code model building is Canvas. Feature storage is Feature Store. Model quality monitoring is Model Monitor.

question: A company needs a solution to convert text into human speech for multilingual audio courses. Which AWS service?

options.A: Amazon Polly
options.B: Amazon Translate
options.C: Amazon Comprehend
options.D: Amazon Lex

rationale: Amazon Polly is a text-to-speech service that synthesizes lifelike human speech in dozens of languages and voices — perfect for creating multilingual audio courses. Translate converts text between languages. Comprehend performs NLP analysis on text. Lex builds conversational chatbot interfaces.

question: An engaging platform wants to teach reinforcement learning through hands-on tools. Which is a CORRECT statement about AWS DeepRacer?

options.A: The AWS DeepRacer vehicle is a Wi-Fi enabled, physical vehicle that can drive itself on a physical track
options.B: You need an AWS DeepRacer car to use the AWS DeepRacer simulator
options.C: AWS DeepRacer vehicle is only a virtual vehicle running on the AWS DeepRacer simulator
options.D: AWS DeepRacer cars are based on a model that uses a supervised learning ML algorithm

rationale: AWS DeepRacer is a Wi-Fi enabled 1/18th scale physical autonomous race car that uses RL models to navigate physical tracks. The simulator is also available separately for virtual training. You do NOT need a physical car to use the simulator (B is false). DeepRacer uses reinforcement learning, not supervised learning (D is false). A physical car does exist (C is false).

question: A media company has intermittent workloads, doesn't want to configure infrastructure, and can tolerate cold starts. Which SageMaker inference option?

options.A: Asynchronous inference
options.B: Real-time hosting services
options.C: Serverless inference
options.D: Batch transform

rationale: SageMaker Serverless Inference automatically provisions and scales infrastructure on demand, charges only when serving requests, and is ideal for intermittent/spiky workloads where you accept cold starts in exchange for cost savings and zero infrastructure management. Real-time hosting maintains persistent endpoints. Asynchronous and batch are for large payload/scheduled jobs.

question: Amazon Personalize is being used to process data from 10 sources. Which AWS service helps import, prepare, and transform data before feeding into Personalize?

options.A: Amazon SageMaker Clarify
options.B: Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler
options.C: Amazon SageMaker Feature Store
options.D: Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth

rationale: SageMaker Data Wrangler is the ETL/data preparation tool that can import data from multiple sources and apply transformations to clean, normalize, and prepare data before it's fed into Amazon Personalize or other ML services. Clarify detects bias. Feature Store stores/serves ML features. Ground Truth labels data.

question: A wildlife research organization has camera trap images of animal species. Which approach identifies and categorizes them?

options.A: Face recognition, specifically designed to identify faces
options.B: Thermal imaging, detecting heat patterns
options.C: Named entity recognition for identifying entities like names, places, or dates
options.D: Object detection, which involves identifying and locating specific objects within an image

rationale: Object detection is the correct computer vision technique for identifying and categorizing multiple animal species within images — it can locate and classify multiple objects in a single image with bounding boxes. Face recognition is specialized for human faces. Thermal imaging detects heat signatures. Named entity recognition processes text, not images.

question: A company needs automated extraction of insights from legal briefs such as contracts and court records. What do you recommend?

options.A: Amazon Rekognition
options.B: Amazon Translate
options.C: Amazon Textract
options.D: Amazon Comprehend

rationale: Amazon Comprehend is an NLP service that automatically extracts insights from text — entities, key phrases, relationships, sentiment, and topics — making it ideal for analyzing legal documents like contracts and court records. Textract extracts raw text from scanned documents (a useful first step). Rekognition is computer vision. Translate converts languages.

question: For Amazon SageMaker Automatic Model Tuning (AMT), which configuration is mandatory?

options.A: Tuning strategy
options.B: None of the listed items
options.C: Number of jobs
options.D: Hyperparameter ranges

rationale: Hyperparameter ranges are mandatory for SageMaker Automatic Model Tuning — you must specify which hyperparameters to tune and their valid search ranges (min, max, scaling type) so the tuning job knows the search space. Tuning strategy has a default (Bayesian). Number of jobs has a default. Hyperparameter ranges must always be explicitly specified.

question: A retail company needs to forecast foot traffic, visitor counts, and channel demand to manage operating costs. Which AWS ML service?

options.A: Amazon Personalize
options.B: Amazon SageMaker Feature Store
options.C: Amazon Forecast
options.D: Amazon Textract

rationale: Amazon Forecast is a time-series forecasting service that uses ML to accurately predict future values like foot traffic, visitor counts, and channel demand. It's purpose-built for business time-series forecasting with minimal ML expertise required. Personalize is for recommendations. Feature Store manages ML features. Textract extracts document text.

question: Match: A) Amazon Textract, B) Amazon Forecast, C) Amazon Kendra. 1) Enterprise search powered by ML, 2) Automatically extract text and data from documents, 3) Forecast business outcomes using ML
rationale: Amazon Textract (A) = automatically extract text, handwriting, tables, and data from any document (A2). Amazon Forecast (B) = forecast business outcomes using ML (B3). Amazon Kendra (C) = easy-to-use enterprise search service powered by machine learning (C1). This is the correct service-to-use-case mapping.

question: What can Amazon Q Developer do to enhance a software development team's efficiency?

options.A: Create SageMaker models for ML development
options.B: Suggest code snippets based on specific tasks or requirements
options.C: Deploy applications by automating the entire deployment process
options.D: Create LLM chatbots for deployment

rationale: Amazon Q Developer's core capability is providing AI-powered code suggestions and completions within IDEs and the AWS Management Console, based on the developer's context, comments, and requirements. It's an AI coding assistant. It doesn't create SageMaker models, autonomously deploy applications, or build chatbots independently.

question: Why is it important to maintain data lineage in ML models on AWS?

options.A: It improves model performance by optimizing data processing
options.B: It enhances the visualization capabilities of the data
options.C: It ensures data privacy and compliance by tracking the flow and transformation of data
options.D: It reduces storage costs by efficiently managing data

rationale: Data lineage tracks the origin, flow, and transformation of data throughout the ML pipeline — providing the audit trail required for regulatory compliance, data privacy requirements, and debugging. When questions arise about model behavior or compliance, data lineage shows exactly where data came from, how it was processed, and who accessed it.

question: A traffic monitoring application needs to detect license plate numbers at night. Which ML-powered AWS service?

options.A: Amazon SageMaker JumpStart
options.B: Amazon Textract
options.C: Amazon SageMaker image classification algorithm
options.D: Amazon Rekognition

rationale: Amazon Rekognition provides text-in-image detection (OCR) that can extract license plate characters from camera footage in real time. JumpStart is a model hub for SageMaker. Textract is for extracting text from scanned documents, not real-time video/camera feeds. SageMaker image classification classifies images but doesn't extract text from them.

question: A company needs to understand the difference between Large Language Models (LLMs) and Small Language Models (SLMs). Which statement best describes this difference?

options.A: LLMs and SLMs are identical in capability and size but differ only in name
options.B: LLMs have billions of parameters trained on massive datasets and excel at general-purpose tasks; SLMs have fewer parameters, are more efficient, and are optimized for specific tasks or resource-constrained environments
options.C: SLMs always outperform LLMs on every task because they are more specialized
options.D: LLMs can only be used for text generation; SLMs can only be used for image generation

rationale: LLMs (e.g., GPT-4, Claude) have billions of parameters trained on massive, diverse datasets — they excel at general-purpose reasoning, generation, and understanding across many domains. SLMs (e.g., Phi, TinyLlama) have significantly fewer parameters, are computationally efficient, and are optimized for specific tasks or deployment in resource-constrained environments like edge devices. Neither is universally 'better' — the choice depends on the use case and constraints.
